inline_comments.message: I know that it is not your change, but please remove unused import
Please add info about csr data. You can get it from 'configuration'
Please rename to e.g 'Cert Service URL' / 'Request URL'

5. Use the imperative mood in the subject line
https://chris.beams.io/posts/git-commit/#imperative
Consider moving it to end of createCsrInPem method.

With layout like this if any exception occured it would log that KeyPair has been generated succcessfully and then that Generation failed.
Consider changing the message to "Generating KeyPair with encryptionAlgorithm"

Also consider using logger string formatter (applies everywhere):
LOGGER.info("Generating KeyPair with encryption algorithm: {}", encryptionAlhorithm);

Similar to KeyPair.
Consider moving to next line or line 49.
I think this should be moved to line 73 because Exceptions could occurred during building.
I think there was such comment. It could be case where this will not be a true. Mark here just that creation was started or something similar.
move logger to be a first
Only for logging purposes so consider moving this to CsrConfiguration toString method.
Consider moving this to toString method of configuration.

Improve readability for example: 

String logDetails = String.format("%s= %s, %s= %s, %s= %s, %s= %s", 
                ClientConfigurationEnvs.REQUEST_URL, configuration.getUrlToCertService(),
                ClientConfigurationEnvs.REQUEST_TIMEOUT, configuration.getRequestTimeout(), 
                ClientConfigurationEnvs.OUTPUT_PATH, ClientConfigurationEnvs.CA_NAME, 
                configuration.getCertsOutputPath(), configuration.getCaName());

When you do it you will see the following:

OUTPUT_PATH = CA_NAME
getCertsOutputPath() = getCaName()


Fix these bugs :)

Replace the concatenation with the following:

LOGGER.error("Generation of KeyPair failed, exception message: {}", e.getMessage());
In my opinion, Logger should be on top of the declared fields.
I think that this order is correct. Public field before the private one.
I think PEM_OBJECT_TYPE access should actually be private. Can you fix it and then move it down so that the logger is first?

It's not about private/public fields. 

A logger is not a part of business logic. 
It's rather a cross-cutting concern (aspect), so it should be distinguished from other class fields.

I strongly recommend to place it on top.
Please log also exception stack trace
Sorry, but sentence is not logical

Two comments:
Why SANS is hardcoded?
Why all these envs are coming from EncryptionAlgorithmConstants?
After look into this code once again I think the better way there is to use CsrConfigurationEnvs. Thanks that there will be no longer hardcoded SANS.
Lets keep it more formal. Replace it with "readability of logs for positive path"

Make it inline like this:
```
PKCS10CertificationRequest request = createPKCS10Csr(subject, keyPair);
```
I would add log in this method on info that conversion of csr to pem is started.

I would log here that "Successful validation of Client configuration. Configuration data: {}"

Also pay attention, you have CsrConfigurationFactory with similar method which needs something similar.

General comment: 
1. Look at each method name 
2. Think what it does
3. Verify if it does what it says
4. than log what you observed
"Conversion of CSR to PEM has been started"
